result:
## What was actually wrong

Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant, but the migration was left half-finished in the code:

1. **`app/layout.tsx` shipped the old product's title/description/OG tags** on every single page ("InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"). This is root-level metadata, so it would have overridden every page's SERP snippet — actively telling Google and searchers the site is something it isn't.
2. **`/features` and `/pricing` didn't exist in the repo** despite being live, high-intent commercial pages.
3. **No blog post route existed** (`app/blog/[slug]`) — the 12 posts driving nearly all of the site's GSC impressions (post-1 alone: 3,900) had no page in this codebase, and the blog index called a `/api/posts` route that didn't exist either.
4. **`sitemap.ts` explicitly excluded blog posts** (a literal `// TODO` stub) and also omitted features/pricing — so the pages actually getting found by Google weren't even being submitted for indexing.
5. **`/faq` 301-redirected to `/#faq`**, an anchor that doesn't exist anywhere — a dead link in the nav and footer of every page.
6. **Total content/keyword mismatch**: GSC shows 100% of impressions on legacy email-cleanup queries (inbox zero, Gmail storage, bulk-delete) and **zero** visibility for anything Lumina sells (meeting notes, transcription, action items).

*(Note: the live URL you gave me was unreachable during this session — the harness process that serves it blocks on a synchronous spawn while I'm running, so it can't answer HTTP requests concurrently. I read the served files directly off disk instead and verified everything against a real local `next build && next start`.)*

## What I shipped
- Fixed root metadata + added Organization/SoftwareApplication JSON-LD (`app/layout.tsx`)
- Built `/features` and `/pricing` with proper per-page metadata
- Built `app/blog/[slug]` (statically generated, per-post metadata/canonical/OG) + `/api/posts` + converted the blog index to server-rendered (was client-spinner-only, invisible to crawlers without JS)
- Fixed `sitemap.ts` to include features, pricing, faq, and all 14 blog posts
- Replaced the dead `/faq` redirect with a real FAQ page + FAQPage schema, removed the redirect from `next.config.js`
- Added contextual "bridge" CTAs (inbox chaos → meeting chaos → Lumina) on the 6 highest-impression legacy posts, rather than rewriting them off-topic and losing their existing rankings
- Seeded the *actual* content gap with 2 new on-target posts (meeting minutes, meeting action items) as the first entries in a real topical cluster

Verified with a clean `npm run build` (24 static routes, all 14 posts) and a local `next start` smoke test — correct titles, JSON-LD, 200s on every new route, sitemap includes everything.

## Next steps (content roadmap)
The technical fixes stop the bleeding; they don't create new rankings. GSC visibility for Lumina's actual market is currently zero, so the highest-leverage next step is a proper topical cluster around "AI meeting assistant" — core pages (features, pricing, per-integration pages for Zoom/Teams/Slack, comparison pages vs. Otter/Fireflies) feeding from AOR content (meeting minutes, action items — started — plus meeting agendas, async-meeting reduction, remote-team communication, decision logs). Happy to run this properly through the topical-authority-map skill with you interactively (it has real checkpoints worth your input on positioning/persona), or keep extending the pattern I started in `app/lib/posts.ts` — your call.

One unrelated flag: `npm install` reports Next.js 14.2.5 has a known security vulnerability (patch available) — worth a dependency bump when convenient, separate from this SEO work.
